verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein primarily resident in the ER membrane, where its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain faces the ER lumen and its C-terminus is cytoplasmic. However, a genuine plasma membrane pool (STIM1PM) with an extracellular N-terminus has been confirmed by N-linked glycosylation analysis, flow cytometry, and ELISA in multiple cell types including cancer cell lines. This PM-localized fraction is a true type I (and dual-topology) surface TM protein with an accessible extracellular domain. The ER-to-PM translocation during store-operated Ca2+ entry (SOCE) positions STIM1 at ER-PM junctions on the cytoplasmic face, which does not contribute to outer-leaflet accessibility. The documented minor PM-resident pool in select cell types qualifies as dual_localization/contextual.
